title: The Old Woman Of Norwich
content:
There was an old woman and what do you think?
    She lived upon nothing but victuals and drink;
    Victuals and drink were the chief of her diet,
    Yet this plaguey old woman could never be quiet.
meaning:
"The Old Woman Of Norwich" is a quintessential example of Walter Crane's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
